creative thinking.explanation_en: the ability to look at things from a new perspective and find new solutions
creative thinking.example_sentence: Creative thinking is essential for solving complex problems at work.

eliminate.explanation_en: to remove or get rid of something completely
eliminate.example_sentence: We need to eliminate all errors from the report before sending it.
eliminate.original_sentence: Eliminate the word impossible from your thinking and speaking vocabularies.

chain reaction.explanation_en: a series of events where one event causes the next one to happen
chain reaction.example_sentence: His negative comment started a chain reaction of complaints from the team.
chain reaction.original_sentence: The thought it's impossible sets off a chain reaction of other thoughts to prove you're right.

whip.explanation_en: in this context, to defeat or overcome something
whip.example_sentence: You have to whip your fear of public speaking to succeed.
whip.original_sentence: Many of us whip and defeat our desires simply because we concentrate on why we can't,

worthy of.example_sentence: This project is worthy of your full attention.
worthy of.original_sentence: when the only thing worthy of our mental concentration is why we can.

cut down.explanation_en: to reduce the amount or size of something
cut down.example_sentence: We need to cut down on our monthly expenses.
cut down.original_sentence: This would cut down greatly on duplicated services so that taxpayers would actually get better service for less money.

stumble across.example_sentence: I stumbled across an old photo while cleaning the attic.
stumble across.original_sentence: The writer of this article said he thought he had stumbled across a really live idea,

at random.explanation_en: without a specific plan or pattern; chosen by chance
at random.example_sentence: The teacher picked students at random to answer the questions.
at random.original_sentence: so he interviewed 30 people at random to get their reactions.

merit.explanation_en: the quality of being good or worthy
merit.example_sentence: His proposal has some merit, but it needs more research.
merit.original_sentence: Not one person thought the idea had merit, even though it would provide them with better local government at less cost.

paralyzed.explanation_en: unable to move or act because of fear or shock
paralyzed.example_sentence: He was paralyzed by fear when he saw the fire.
paralyzed.original_sentence: The traditional thinker's mind is paralyzed.

resent.explanation_en: to feel angry or unhappy about something that you think is unfair
resent.example_sentence: She resented being told what to do by someone younger than her.
resent.original_sentence: Average people have always resented progress.

drastic.example_sentence: The company had to take drastic measures to avoid bankruptcy.
drastic.original_sentence: The airplane seemed drastic to many.

status-quoers.explanation_en: people who want things to stay exactly as they are now
status-quoers.example_sentence: The status-quoers in the office refused to try the new software.
status-quoers.original_sentence: A lot of status-quoers still insist that man has no business in space.

deviate.explanation_en: to change from a planned path or standard
deviate.example_sentence: It is important not to deviate from the instructions provided.
deviate.original_sentence: Find the best way, then never deviate from it.

financial ruin.explanation_en: the state of having lost all one's money or business success
financial ruin.example_sentence: Poor management led the company to financial ruin.
financial ruin.original_sentence: Fortunately for this man's company, new leadership came in in time to save the organization from financial ruin.

rear.explanation_en: to take care of a child until they are fully grown
rear.example_sentence: They worked hard to rear their children in a safe environment.
rear.original_sentence: make a sail, rear a child, or cook a steak.

sprout.explanation_en: to start to grow or develop
sprout.example_sentence: New ideas began to sprout after the brainstorming session.
sprout.original_sentence: If we let tradition freeze our minds, new ideas can't sprout.
